title: Campus Circular Mart – College Second-Hand Marketplace Frontend

requirements.summary: Implement Internet Identity-based authentication flow with conditional UI for unauthenticated users
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users can browse the listings page
Clicking Post a Listing or Contact Seller redirects unauthenticated users to a login prompt
After login, the user is returned to their intended action
Navbar displays login/logout button and logged-in user state

file_operations.description: Create a modal component that prompts first-time users to enter their display name after login. Use the authorization component to check if the user has a profile via getCallerUserProfile(). If null, show the modal with a form to collect displayName and save it via saveCallerUserProfile(). Prevent modal flash by properly checking actor and profile loading states. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the ProfileSetupModal into the main app layout. Use the authorization component's useInternetIdentity hook to check authentication state. Render the ProfileSetupModal when isAuthenticated is true, actor is ready, profile is fetched, and userProfile is null. Ensure the modal appears after login but before the user can interact with protected features. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable authentication guard component that wraps children and shows a login prompt when unauthenticated users attempt to access protected features. Accept a 'requiredAuth' prop (boolean). Use the authorization component's useInternetIdentity hook to check authentication state. If requiredAuth is true and user is not authenticated, render a login prompt with a message and login button. Otherwise render children. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build responsive Listings Browse page with search, category filters, and card grid layout
acceptanceCriteria: All active listings are displayed as cards in a responsive grid
Each card shows image or placeholder, title, price, and category badge
Search bar filters listings in real-time by title or description
Category chips filter listings to show only matching category
Selecting All removes the category filter
An empty state is shown when no listings match the current filters

file_operations.description: Create the main Browse Listings page component. Fetch all listings via getAllListings() from the backend. Implement local state for search text and selected category filter. Filter the listings array in real-time based on search (matching title or description case-insensitively) and category. Render a search input, category filter chips (All, Textbooks, Electronics, Clothing, Furniture, Stationery, Other), and a responsive card grid. Show an empty state message when the filtered array is empty. Each listing card must display the listing image (use frontend/public/assets/generated/listing-placeholder.dim_400x300.png as fallback), title, price formatted as currency, and a category badge. Make each card clickable to navigate to the listing detail page.

Create a reusable ListingCard component that accepts a listing object as a prop. Display the listing image (or frontend/public/assets/generated/listing-placeholder.dim_400x300.png if imageId is missing), title, price (formatted as currency), and category badge. Apply campus-friendly styling with rounded corners, subtle shadow, and hover effects. Use the blob-storage component's ExternalBlob.fromURL(blob.getDirectURL()) to render listing images when imageId is present. The card should be clickable and emit an onClick event. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a CategoryBadge component that accepts a category prop (Textbooks | Electronics | Clothing | Furniture | Stationery | Other). Render a small badge with the category name. Apply distinct muted colors per category: Textbooks (blue), Electronics (purple), Clothing (pink), Furniture (brown), Stationery (yellow), Other (gray). Use rounded corners and padding for a pill-shaped badge appearance.

Create a reusable EmptyState component that displays a centered message when no listings match the current filters. Accept a 'message' prop (string) with a default value of 'No listings found'. Render an icon (e.g., empty box or magnifying glass) and the message text in a centered, friendly layout.

requirements.summary: Build authenticated Post Listing form with image upload and validation
acceptanceCriteria: Form is only accessible to authenticated users; unauthenticated users see a login prompt
All fields (title, description, price, category, photo) are present
Submitting with missing required fields shows validation errors
On successful submission, the new listing appears on the Browse page
The uploaded image is displayed on the listing

file_operations.description: Create the Post Listing form page. Wrap the entire page content in the AuthGuard component with requiredAuth=true to ensure only authenticated users can access it. Render a form with fields: title (text input), description (textarea), price (numeric input), category (dropdown with all six categories), and photo upload (file input accepting images). Implement client-side validation for required fields (title, description, price, category). On form submission, use the blob-storage component's ExternalBlob.fromBytes() to upload the selected image file, track upload progress with withUploadProgress(), then call createListing() with the uploaded imageId. Use the user's profile displayName as sellerDisplayName. Show validation errors inline. On success, navigate to the Browse page and invalidate the listings query cache. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build Listing Detail page with Contact Seller feature for authenticated users
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a listing card opens the detail page or modal
Full listing details are displayed including image, title, description, price, category, seller name, and timestamp
Contact Seller button is visible; unauthenticated users are prompted to log in
Authenticated users see the seller display name and a contact message on clicking the button

file_operations.description: Create the Listing Detail page component. Accept a listingId route parameter. Fetch the full listing data from the listings query (or refetch getAllListings and find by ID). Display the listing image (use frontend/public/assets/generated/listing-placeholder.dim_400x300.png if missing), title, description, price, category badge (using CategoryBadge component), seller display name, and formatted timestamp. Include a Contact Seller button. Use the authorization component to check if the user is authenticated. If not authenticated, clicking the button shows the AuthGuard login prompt. If authenticated, reveal the seller's display name and a pre-formatted contact message (e.g., 'Contact [sellerDisplayName] about this listing'). Use the blob-storage component's ExternalBlob.fromURL(blob.getDirectURL()) to render the listing image. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build authenticated My Listings page with delete functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Page is only accessible to authenticated users
Displays all listings owned by the logged-in user
Each listing shows thumbnail, title, price, category badge, and Delete button
Deleting a listing removes it from the backend and updates the UI immediately
An empty state is shown if the user has no listings

file_operations.description: Create the My Listings page. Wrap the page content in the AuthGuard component with requiredAuth=true. Fetch the current user's listings via getMyListings() from the backend. Render each listing as a card or row showing the listing image thumbnail (use frontend/public/assets/generated/listing-placeholder.dim_400x300.png as fallback), title, price, category badge (using CategoryBadge component), and a Delete button. On clicking Delete, call deleteListing(listingId) and immediately invalidate/refetch the listings query to update the UI. Show the EmptyState component with message 'You have no listings yet' when the array is empty. Use the blob-storage component's ExternalBlob.fromURL(blob.getDirectURL()) to render listing thumbnails. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build responsive Navbar with app branding, navigation links, and authentication controls
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar shows logo, Browse, Post a Listing, and My Listings links
Login button shown for unauthenticated users; logout and user identifier shown for authenticated users
Navbar collapses to a hamburger menu on mobile screens
Navigation links route to correct pages

file_operations.description: Create a responsive Navbar component. Display the app logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/campus-circular-mart-logo.dim_256x256.png) and the text 'Campus Circular Mart' on the left. Add navigation links to Browse, Post a Listing, and My Listings. Use the authorization component's useInternetIdentity hook to display login/logout state: show a Login button for unauthenticated users, and show the user's display name (from profile) and a Logout button for authenticated users. On mobile screens, collapse the navigation links into a hamburger menu. Use React Router's Link or NavLink components for navigation. Clear the query cache on logout as per component usage instructions. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire the Navbar component into the main app layout. Render the Navbar at the top of the application, above the router outlet. Ensure the Navbar is visible on all pages.

requirements.summary: Apply campus-friendly visual theme with campus green primary color and warm neutral accents
acceptanceCriteria: Campus green is the primary brand color used in buttons, badges, and accents
Warm neutral tones are used for page backgrounds
All listing cards have rounded corners and subtle drop shadows
Each category has a visually distinct badge color
The theme is applied consistently across all pages and components
The UI is fully responsive on mobile, tablet, and desktop

file_operations.description: Update the global styles to apply the campus-friendly visual theme. Define CSS custom properties for campus green (primary color, e.g., #2D5016 or similar), cream/light tan for backgrounds, and muted colors for category badges. Set body background to a warm neutral tone. Apply the theme consistently to all buttons, links, and UI elements. Ensure all styles support responsive layouts.

Extend the Tailwind configuration to include the campus-friendly color palette. Add campus green as the primary color, warm neutral tones (cream, light tan) for backgrounds, and distinct muted colors for each of the six categories (Textbooks, Electronics, Clothing, Furniture, Stationery, Other). Configure rounded corners and shadow utilities to match the design requirements.
